findings.message: 'prepare': 'husky' runs automatically on npm install; legitimate husky pattern but satisfies postinstall-script HIGH pattern.
Most devDependencies use ^ ranges allowing automatic minor/patch resolution; supply-chain drift can introduce vulnerabilities between lockfile updates.
Command declares no allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot restrict tool usage for this command.
No output format specified; success and error messages shown to the user after login are undefined.
Command prompts for API key but does not specify behavior when invoked with no key and REFLY_API_KEY env var is unset.
Command declares no allowed-tools field.
Command declares no allowed-tools field.
No output format specified; what Claude tells the user after the upgrade completes is undefined.

SKILL.md References section cites 'rules/execution.md' etc. matching the deployed path (~/.refly/skills/base/rules/) but source files live at packages/cli/skill/references/; upgrade/install must map references/ to rules/ correctly or references silently break.
new Function(`return ${str}`)() executes translation file content as JavaScript; a malicious translation file could run arbitrary code in the developer's environment.
REFLY_REPO env var controls the git clone URL; an attacker who controls the environment before running the script can redirect the clone to a malicious repository.
MINIO_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_KEY and MINIO_EXTERNAL_SECRET_KEY read from env and passed to external MinIO client; misconfigured or leaked env vars expose storage credentials.
Full database connection URL (with embedded credentials) read from REFLY_DATABASE_URL_LOCAL; URL is easily captured by logging or error output.
Command declares no allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot restrict tool usage for this command.
No output format specified; success and error messages shown to the user after login are undefined.
Command prompts for API key but does not specify behavior when invoked with no key and REFLY_API_KEY env var is unset.
Command declares no allowed-tools field.
Command declares no allowed-tools field.
No output format specified; what Claude tells the user after the upgrade completes is undefined.
new Function(`return ${str}`)() executes translation file content as JavaScript; a malicious translation file could run arbitrary code in the developer's environment.
